**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 48 **Prerequisites** [Tsukumogami Poppet](/Heritages.aspx?ID=383) heritage --- You can use your body as a deadly weapon. You gain one of the following melee unarmed attacks, which must be one that matches your body (for instance, tsukumogami wrap for a tsukumogami that’s a bolt of cotton).
• A blade unarmed attack that deals 1d6 slashing, has the [versatile](/Traits.aspx?ID=724) P trait, and is in the [sword](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=15) weapon group.
• A spoke unarmed attack that deals 1d4 piercing, has the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) and [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602) traits, and is in the [knife](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=9) weapon group.
• A wrap unarmed attack that deals 1d4 bludgeoning, has the [grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), and [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716) traits, and is in the [flail](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=7) weapon group. **Special** You can take this feat only at 1st level, and you can't retrain into or out of this feat.
name: Made for Combat

>
[Sudden Terror](/Feats.aspx?ID=6873) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Poppet" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=418" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 48

**Prerequisites**
[Tsukumogami Poppet](/Heritages.aspx?ID=383) heritage

</column>

---

You leverage your body’s amorphous nature to spook others. Attempt to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) a creature within 30 feet. Demoralize loses the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait and gains the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) trait, and you don’t take a penalty if the creature doesn’t understand your language. If you were hidden to the creature, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your check, and if you succeed, the creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2 instead of frightened 1. All creatures that witnessed you use Sudden Terror are then temporarily immune to your Sudden Terror for 24 hours.
name: Sudden Terror

    right="Feat 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[No Hands, No Problems](/Feats.aspx?ID=6876) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Poppet" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=418" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 49

**Prerequisites**
[Tsukumogami Poppet](/Heritages.aspx?ID=383) heritage

</column>

---

After a lifetime of using temporary, ghostly limbs to manipulate objects, you hardly need real hands. You can extend your limbs to perform simple [Interact](/Rules.aspx?ID=2151) actions at a range 5 feet beyond your normal range, after which your limbs contract back to their normal length. You can also cast the [_telekinetic hand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) cantrip as an arcane or occult innate spell at will, which always takes the ghostlike form of telekinetic hand and is tethered to your body via a snaking, illusory arm. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up.
name: No Hands, No Problems

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Solidarity](/Feats.aspx?ID=6877) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Poppet" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=418" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 49

**Frequency**
Once per 10 Minutes

**Prerequisites**
[Tsukumogami Poppet](/Heritages.aspx?ID=383) heritage

</column>

---

You bestow some of your own life energies to mend damage. You touch a non-magical object of 2 Bulk or less, or a magical object of 1 Bulk or less, and you restore Hit Points to the target equal to twice your level. You lose as many Hit Points as the target regained. This direct transfer of vitality means that no effects apply that would increase the Hit Points the target regains or decrease the Hit Points you lose. This transfer also ignores any temporary Hit Points you or the target have. You can’t reduce your Hit Points below 1 using Solidarity. You can’t replace lost pieces or repair an object that has been completely destroyed.

**Special** If you have the [Minuscule Mentee](/Feats.aspx?ID=6580) feat, you can use Solidarity to heal your familiar.
name: Solidarity

[Awaken the Obake](/Feats.aspx?ID=6878) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Poppet" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=418" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 49

**Prerequisites**
[Tsukumogami Poppet](/Heritages.aspx?ID=383) heritage

</column>

---

Not every tool is well cared for, and those that are treated badly might awaken as malevolent spirits. You can temporarily evoke the spirit within a nearby mistreated tool to cast [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) as a 5th-rank innate arcane spell once per day. When you do so, the terrifying magic of the spell originates from a single unattended object within 30 feet of you instead of from you, and you measure the spell’s range from that object. If a target becomes [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) as a result of the spell, they’re fleeing from that object instead of from you as their mind fills with the absolute certainty that the object will come to life and attack them with malicious glee.
name: Awaken the Obake

You’ve trained to use unique nagaji eating habits to your advantage. By manipulating your throat muscles, you can create a pocket of air to store up to four items of light Bulk or less without risk of dissolving. None of these items can have a dimension longer than 1 foot. As long as you have at least one item in your throat pocket, your speech is noticeably difficult to understand, and you have a visible bulge in your neck. Placing an item in your throat pocket or retrieving one is an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action. You can empty your throat pocket with a single action, causing everything you had stored in it to fall to the ground in your square. Any item that was covered in dirt or grime comes out clean.

</traits>

You rejuvenate your spirit by taking your rest where Mother Earth embraces Father Moon. When you rest outdoors, you can choose one of the following benefits, which lasts until your next sleep cycle and can’t be changed.

**Father Moon’s Vigilance** While sleeping, you don’t take a penalty to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) rolls for being [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95).

**Mother Earth’s Care** After 8 hours of sleep, you regain additional Hit Points equal to your Constitution modifier plus your level.
name: Rejuvenating Embrace

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Convocation of Earth and Moon](/Feats.aspx?ID=6928) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=662" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />
<trait label="Sarangay" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=829" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 63

**Frequency**
once per day

</column>

---

You call upon Mother Earth and Father Moon to fill you with light. A cocoon of moonlight momentarily envelops you, and you emerge in a towering form made of swirling energy, tattooed in stardust and clad in regalia of silver and mother of pearl. When you use this ability, you gain the benefits of both the 4th-rank [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1514) and [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534) spells. This form lasts for 5 minutes or until you use this action again to return to your normal form.

If you have your head gem, it radiates an aura of moonlight, causing creatures that start their turn adjacent to you to become [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the beginning of their turn unless they succeed at a Will save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher.
name: Convocation of Earth and Moon

You know how to leverage your size and momentum when using your horns attack, and can use your movement speed to overrun your foes. You [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to double your Speed and can move through the spaces of creatures at least one size smaller than you, Trampling each creature whose space you enter. You can attempt to Trample the same creature only once in a single Trample. You deal piercing damage equal to the damage of your horns unarmed attack against these creatures, which can attempt a basic Reflex save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC.

There’s nothing better than being a tanuki, but sometimes you need a less conspicuous form when going into the big city or other crowded places. When you [Change Shape](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=90), you can assume the form of a common Small or Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) ancestry prevalent where you grew up, typically human. This everyday form is a specific form with the same age and body type as your true tanuki form and has roughly analogous physical traits, such as hair color.

<title level="2">Everyday Form leads to...</title>
[False Priest Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=6935), [Many Faces](/Feats.aspx?ID=6940)

Your flat check to remove [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) is DC 10 instead of DC 15, which can be reduced to DC 5 with appropriate assistance. Your shapeshifting is advanced enough to take the form of not just creatures, but objects as well. When you Change Shape, you can assume the form of a simple tool or object of 1 Bulk or less, such as a teakettle or umbrella. Nobody respects tanuki, but most everyone respects an esteemed priest, so what better form to take if you want to get by a little easier? When you Change Shape, you can assume the shape of a priest or other religious official from a religion of your choosing. While in your false priest form, you can attempt [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about Religion, and you can cast [_divine lance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498) and [_haunting hymn_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1999) as [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) [innate](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) cantrips. If you critically fail at a Deception check while in your false priest form, your transformation is broken, returning you to your tanuki form, and you’re so embarrassed that you can’t resume your False Priest Form until your next daily preparations, after you’ve taken a night to sleep on it.
name: False Priest Form

You know how to run an enterprise that others would never be able to get off the ground—some say it’s your savvy acumen or jovial manner, but you know it’s because you can cut costs by transforming leaves into money. You can use [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) to [Earn Income](/Rules.aspx?ID=2635), and you gain a +1 status bonus to your check if you do so. The illusion doesn’t hold long, though! The GM rolls a secret d20; that many hours after concluding your check, the money transforms back, likely inviting consequences depending on how far the bills traveled from you in the time they were transformed.
name: Leaf Transformation

---

An inconspicuous statue in front of a shop is the perfect form to wait for things to blow over. When you Change Shape, you can assume the form of a statue, shop sign, or other heavy object of up to Large size. Using statue form counts as setting up a disguise for the [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) use of [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), except that you can Impersonate an object instead of a creature, it gives you a +4 status bonus to Deception checks to prevent others from seeing through your disguise, and you add your level even if you’re untrained. Your weight increases to match the object you’re impersonating while in statue form, allowing you to use your spell DC or your class DC instead of the relevant DC to resist forced movement (usually Fortitude), if they’re higher. You can speak while in statue form, but you can’t attack, cast spells, or move.
name: Statue Form

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />
<trait label="Tanuki" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=830" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 68

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The Combined Form tanuki feat overrides the normal restrictions that a target can only be under the effect of one polymorph effect at a time._

**Frequency**
Once per hour

**Trigger**
An ally within 30 feet uses a [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) effect.

</column>

---

Cooperation and coordination are the secrets to getting by in tanuki society. When the triggering ally transforms, you Stride to their square and join shape with them, melding into their transformation. While you’re melded in this way, you grant them the effects of either a 2nd- or 4th‑rank [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1514) spell as well as temporary Hit Points equal to your level; if their [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) effect would normally grant them temporary Hit Points, you increase the amount by your level instead. While you’re melded, you can’t be separately targeted and you can’t act except to speak or to use Change Shape to exit the Combined Form, which ends the effects of enlarge on your ally and removes any remaining temporary Hit Points they received from this ability at the time. If the triggering ally becomes [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) or the triggering polymorph effect ends, you automatically exit the Combined Form.
name: Combined Form

</column>

---

While walking down the road on a moonlit night, one might be puzzled to find a shop or hill that wasn’t there before. In reality, this is a tanuki at the height of their power. When you Change Shape, you can transform into a building or terrain feature up to 50 feet tall and up to 50 feet on a side. Mundane features of your landscape form function as normal—for instance, if you transform into a hot spring, the hot spring contains relaxing hot water, and if you transform into an inn, the inn contains bedrooms, beds, and delicious food—though no aspect of your landscape form can be an active hazard that can cause damage or impose conditions, and any objects created within your landscape form turn into leaves if taken outside of it. You can speak while in landscape form, but you can’t attack, cast spells, or move. You can remain in landscape form for up to 8 hours, and when you exit landscape form, it dissolves around anyone who was inside of it.
name: Landscape Form

No distance can stop tanuki from making their way to a good party. You whistle and point, and 808 tanuki, in their raccoon dog forms, somehow appear from the surrounding terrain and fill a 30-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 120 feet. The tanuki scurry over the ground, leap through the air, and climb up walls and surfaces, dealing 6d8 bludgeoning damage to all enemies in the area as they’re trampled and danced upon. The tanuki continue to party in the area for the next minute, dealing 3d8 bludgeoning damage to any enemy that ends its turn in the area and transforming the area into [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) (though the tanuki allow you and your allies to pass normally). Creatures in raccoon dog form can attempt to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) within the mass of tanuki. After 1 minute, the tanuki clap 30 times and run off to the next party. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the effect, though if you do, the tanuki grumble as the party ends early.
name: Start the Festival!

**Activate—Guide My Dreams** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** You ask the spirit within the pusaka to show you the way. You cast [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549) as an [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662) cantrip. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up.

</column>

---

You take on the shape you had before you awoke to hide in plain sight. When you Change Shape, you can assume the form of an immobile plant, object, or natural feature of Tiny, Small, or Medium size, as suits your heritage. Using your immobile form counts as setting up a disguise for the [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) use of [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), except that you can Impersonate an object instead of a creature, it gives you a +4 status bonus to Deception checks to prevent others from seeing through your disguise, and you add your level even if you’re untrained. You can speak while in immobile form, but you can’t attack, cast spells, or move.
name: Immobile Form
prerequisite_markdown: [Born of Item](/Heritages.aspx?ID=415) heritage, [Born of Elements](/Heritages.aspx?ID=414) heritage, or [Born of Vegetation](/Heritages.aspx?ID=416) heritage

[Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) the target using the unarmed attack associated with your current elemental stance, then Cycle Elemental Stance. You’ve trained in battle formations for a plethora of situations. Choose a formation. Allies within your aura can use their reaction to Stride up to their Speed and take a place in the chosen formation. Allies can’t benefit from this movement if there isn’t room for them in the chosen formation. If there are more eligible allies than there are available spaces in the chosen formation, you choose which allies benefit.

**Line** Your allies gather adjacent to you and each other to form a straight line in a direction of your choosing. 

**Wedge** Your allies gather into any space that would be covered by a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) originating from you in a direction of your choosing. 

**Cluster** Your allies gather into any space that would be covered by a 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) in an aura around you.

You’re a warrior who trains your spirit and body to work in perfect harmony, enhancing your attacks with your spiritual energy while fighting with a ferocious martial technique that combines blade and fist. The damage die for your fist changes to 1d6 instead of 1d4, and your fist gains the [parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667) trait. You don’t take the normal –2 circumstance penalty when making a lethal attack with your fist or any other unarmed attacks. You gain the Overwhelming Combination action.

**Activate—Overwhelming Combination** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=606))   **Requirements** You’re wielding a one-handed melee weapon or a melee weapon with the agile or finesse trait; **Effect** Make two [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against a target within your reach, one with the required weapon and one with your fist [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) attack. If both hit the same target, combine their damage for the purposes of its [resistances](/Rules.aspx?ID=2893) and weaknesses. Apply your multiple attack penalty to each Strike normally.

<title level="2">Spirit Warrior Dedication leads to...</title>
[Cutting Heaven, Crushing Earth](/Feats.aspx?ID=7024), [Flowing Palm Deflection](/Feats.aspx?ID=7025), [Gods' Palm](/Feats.aspx?ID=7028), [Intercepting Hand](/Feats.aspx?ID=7031), [Kaiju Defense Oath](/Feats.aspx?ID=7021), [Sacred Wilds Oath](/Feats.aspx?ID=7022), [Sheltering Pulse](/Feats.aspx?ID=7029), [Spirit of the Blade](/Feats.aspx?ID=7026), [Sword of Sealing](/Feats.aspx?ID=7032), [Sword-light Wave](/Feats.aspx?ID=7027), [Transcendent Deflection](/Feats.aspx?ID=7030), [Tricksterbane Oath](/Feats.aspx?ID=7023)

Attacks made as part of your Overwhelming Combination ability gain a +4 circumstance bonus to damage against a creature at least 2 sizes larger than you, or +6 if you have master proficiency with the weapon you used. <title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Starlit Sentinel Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7033) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 94

**Archetype** [Starlit Sentinel](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=275)

</column>

---

You’ve been chosen by one of the constellations of the Tian Xia zodiac. You gain a transformation seal: a mundane-seeming item of light Bulk, such as a ring, brooch, or key, that has the [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) trait. If your seal is ever lost or destroyed, you can gain a replacement by spending 1 week of downtime in introspection to reconnect with your constellation. You, and only you, can transform into your sentinel form by Activating the seal.

**Activate—Starlit Transformation** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Light swirls around you, transforming your armor, clothing, and a single weapon in your possession into a specific alternate outfit. While your equipment might look dramatically different, it functions as normal. If someone attempts to discern your identity, they must use a [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) action to attempt a [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) check against your [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) DC, as if you were using the [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) action. Your Deception DC against such attempts is 20 + your proficiency modifier instead of the normal DC. Unlike with Impersonate, you don’t have to attempt a Deception check to interact with someone to conceal your identity—a check happens only if someone else specifically tries to uncover it. You remain in your sentinel form for 10 minutes or until you use Starlit Transformation again to change back to your normal form. 

While you're in sentinel form, your transformed weapon shines with starlight and gains a +1 status bonus to damage rolls with the weapon. You can fling bolts of starlight from your weapon with a Strike action, using your melee attack modifier with the weapon. These bolts deal 1d4 force damage, have a range of 60 feet, are affected by your weapon runes, and have the [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) and [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610) traits.

<title level="2">Starlit Sentinel Dedication leads to...</title>
[Blade of the Heart](/Feats.aspx?ID=7036), [Desperate Wish](/Feats.aspx?ID=7037), [Majestic Proclamation](/Feats.aspx?ID=6741), [Sentinel's Orbit](/Feats.aspx?ID=7038), [Special Sentinel Technique](/Feats.aspx?ID=7034)
name: Starlit Sentinel Dedication

You can channel the power of your constellation into a unique technique. You gain either the [_luminous stardust healing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2132) or [_shining starlight attack_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2133) [focus](/Rules.aspx?ID=285) spell, which you can cast only in sentinel form. When you gain this feat, decide a name for your technique, which becomes the spell's incantation. 

If you don't already have one, you gain a focus pool of 1 Focus Point, which you can Refocus by spending 10 minutes outside of your sentinel form to reflect on the values of your constellation. Starlit sentinel focus spells are [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) spells. You become trained in spell attack modifier and spell DC, and your spellcasting ability for these spells is Charisma.

**Special** You can take this feat a second time, gaining the focus spell that you didn’t gain the first time.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=590" />

</column>

---

The bonds of the heart are stronger than any steel, more powerful than any magic. You plunge your transformed weapon into the heart of a willing adjacent ally, where it phases harmlessly into their body. As you pull the weapon out, your ally’s heart inscribes one of the following weapon property runes on your weapon: [corrosive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2834), [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838), [frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839), [shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2847), [thundering](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2850), or [vitalizing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2852). The first time you use Blade of the Heart with a given ally, the GM should decide which rune best represents your shared relationship (such as elegant frost for a respected mentor or brash thundering for a boisterous sparring partner); thereafter, each time you use Blade of the Heart with that ally, you draw the same rune. This rune lasts for as long as you remain in sentinel form and counts toward your maximum limit of runes as normal.

At 16th level, you draw the greater version of the rune instead.
name: Blade of the Heart

If the triggering attack misses, you can redirect the ammunition into a nearby pocket or container where it can be retrieved and reused.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wandering Chef Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=7053) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 100

**Archetype** [Wandering Chef](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=277)

</column>

---

Whether trained in a noble kitchen or a humble home, you know how to cook delicious dishes and source ingredients from the wilderness or urban settings. You become trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37); if you were already trained in Crafting, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You can use [cookware](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2717) instead of an [alchemist’s toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2702) to craft alchemical foods. When using the [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368) downtime activity, you can use Crafting or Cooking [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) in place of [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49), and if you roll a failure, you get a success instead.

You gain the [Quick Alchemy](/Rules.aspx?ID=3282) benefits, but can use it only to create [consumables](/Rules.aspx?ID=2951), and the consumables must be [alchemical food](/Rules.aspx?ID=1905). Any items you choose with Alchemical Crafting must be alchemical food, but they can be 1st level or 2nd level instead of only 1st level. 

You create up to 4 [versatile vials](/Classes.aspx?ID=56) during your daily preparations. Typically, a wandering chef’s versatile vials take the form of parcels of foraged ingredients.

<title level="2">Wandering Chef Dedication leads to...</title>
[Additional Servings](/Feats.aspx?ID=7055), [Cosmic Cocktail](/Feats.aspx?ID=7059), [Feast Planner](/Feats.aspx?ID=7057), [Morning Side Dishes](/Feats.aspx?ID=7054), [Packed with Flavor](/Feats.aspx?ID=7058)
name: Wandering Chef Dedication

When you use [advanced alchemy](/Feats.aspx?ID=6185) to create alchemical food, you can designate one infused item to not automatically expire when you next make your daily preparations. **Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 101 **Archetype** [Wandering Chef](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=277) **Prerequisites** [Morning Side Dishes](/Feats.aspx?ID=7054) --- You can create dishes that reflect the food sources endemic to your environment and medicinal food techniques utilizing the cycle of the five elements. When you create alchemical food from Morning Side Dishes during your daily preparations, you can add one elemental benefit of your choice to each of the items. This benefit comes in addition to any other effect of consuming the item. Each element has an immediate benefit and a +3 item bonus to certain saves that lasts for 1 hour after the food is consumed. The item bonus increases to +4 if the alchemical item is 18th level or higher.
  • **Wood** Remove one source of [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). For 1 hour, gain a +3 item bonus to saves against [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) and [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) effects.
  • **Fire** Reduce the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) condition by 1. For 1 hour, gain a +3 item bonus to saves against being [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) or [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76)
  • **Earth** Reduce the [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) condition by 1. For 1 hour, gain a +3 item bonus to saves against being [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71).
  • **Metal** Reduce the [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition by 1. For 1 hour, gain a +3 item bonus to saves against being stupefied.
  • **Water** Reduce the frightened condition by 2. For 1 hour, gain a +3 item bonus to saves against [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) or drained.
name: Regional Specialty

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 106 **Prerequisites** Trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37), Herbalism Lore, or Trained in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) **Access** Tian Xia origin --- You diagnose your patient, then prepare and administer a dose of elemental medicine to a creature. Healer’s tools contain basic ingredients for crafting elemental medicine, though you might also need to gather more potent ingredients from your environment. The medicine’s effects last 24 hours, and no character can benefit from more than one elemental medicine at a time. Attempt a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37), Herbalism [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), or [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) check against a standard DC of the recipient’s level (in the case of especially wellknown or obscure afflictions, the GM should apply an adjustment to lower or increase the DC, respectively). As the minute qualities of the patient’s condition can change on a day-to-day basis, you must attempt a new check to prepare elemental medicine each day you treat a patient, even if the affliction you’re treating doesn’t change. You can diagnose and treat up to 6 creatures during your daily preparations, making a separate check for each patient.
**Critical Success** You accurately diagnose your patient. The GM decides the elemental imbalance causing the patient’s symptoms (which elements, as well as whether it’s in excess or deficiency) and then, using the information from the Pei Zing Elemental Associations table, notifies you what element of medicine is needed to remedy it. With this information, you then accurately identify the elemental property of a given ingredient, and then use it to prepare an excellent elemental medicine that grants the recipient a +2 circumstance bonus to saves against the affliction.
**Success** As critical success, but the bonus is +1.
**Failure** You fail to diagnose your patient. The GM doesn’t tell you what elemental imbalance is causing the patient’s symptoms, and therefore, you can’t prepare an elemental medicine.
**Critical Failure** You misdiagnose your patient. The GM decides the elemental imbalance causing the patient’s symptoms and then tells you a different, false diagnosis or notifies you that the elemental ingredient you’re attempting to treat the symptoms with is actually incorrect. Either way, your mistaken treatment imposes a –1 circumstance penalty to saves against the affliction. In addition, characters who have access to Prepare Elemental Medicine also have the ability to use Crafting, Herbalism Lore, or Medicine to Recall Knowledge about elemental afflictions and associations generally, which they can do even if untrained, as with other uses of [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367). ## Pei Zing Elemental Associations

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=615" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=695" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 108

**Prerequisites**
[Prepare Elemental Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=7065); Expert in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)

**Access**
Minkai or Forest of Spirits origin

</column>

---

You learn Minkaian traditions about the void and know how to manipulate the five elements to fortify vitality and void energies in the body. When you Prepare Elemental Medicine, you can choose to use [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) instead of the usual skills. If you do, the medicine also grants the recipient [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 2 and [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) 2 based on the element you chose. A wood, earth, or water elemental medicine grants resistance to [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage and weakness to [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) damage, and a living recipient loses its [immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2313) to vitality damage for the duration. A fire or metal elemental medicine grants resistance to vitality damage and weakness to void damage, and an undead recipient loses its immunity to void damage for the duration.

If you’re a master in Occultism, increase the resistance and weakness to 4. If you’re legendary, instead increase them to 8.
name: Energy Fortification

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Druid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=583" />
<trait label="Ranger" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=682" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=701" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 109

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42)

**Access**
Tian Xia origin

**Requirements**
You were the recipient of [elemental medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=7065) during your last daily preparations.

</column>

---

You expend the elemental medicine in your body to empower your attacks, though you can’t consume any more elemental medicine until the next day. While in Intensified Element Stance, your strikes and damaging spells deal an additional 1d6 damage against certain creatures, depending on the type of elemental medicine you expended. If the person who crafted the elemental medicine expended was legendary in the skill they used, increase this damage to 2d6. 

**Earth** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) damage against water creatures 

**Fire** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage against metal creatures and constructs that are primarily metallic 

**Metal** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) damage against wood or plant creatures and constructs primarily made of wood or plant matter 

**Water** [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage against fire creatures 

**Wood** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) damage against earth creatures and constructs that are primarily made of rock or earth
name: Intensified Element Stance

<traits>

</traits>

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42)

**Access**
Tian Xia origin

**Requirements**
You and your [eidolon](/Traits.aspx?ID=386) are within 30 feet of one another. 

</column>

---

Many physical poses can fortify one’s health, but some poses need more than one body to accomplish. As you and your eidolon pose in tandem, you each channel qi into a protective barrier in a 30-[emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) until the start of your next turn. Creatures within range of either you or you eidolon gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to your choice of [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage equal to half your level. In addition, any Medicine check targeting a creature with this resistance gets a +1 status bonus (or a +2 status bonus if you’re a master in Medicine).
name: Protective Pose
